cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking how long the experiment lasted according to the text. However, the experiment probably didn't end there because the study also involved a skin biopsy and monitoring the healing process. The next key detail is that "two weeks after the third day of writing, all participants received a standard 4mm skin biopsy..." So the writing phase was three days, then two weeks until the biopsy was done. But then, after the biopsy, the wounds were photographed regularly over the following days to determine the healing rate. The text also says that on the 11th day after the biopsy, the wounds had completely healed for 76.2% of the expressive writing group and 42.1% for the other group.
